executive_summary: Zone B has a critical compound flash-fire risk before the normal hydrocarbon alarm threshold is reached.
risk_explanation: Hydrocarbon concentration is rising around pump P-101 while ventilation is unavailable, a hot-work permit is active, and workers are present. Together these conditions create a credible ignition pathway even though the gas detector has not reached its normal standalone alarm threshold.

recommended_actions.title: Review immediate suspension of hot work
recommended_actions.description: The area supervisor should verify conditions and, if confirmed, suspend the hot-work permit under the site's approved permit procedure.

recommended_actions.title: Account for exposed workers
recommended_actions.description: The shift supervisor should confirm worker locations and apply the approved site evacuation or exclusion procedure if conditions warrant it.

evidence.title: Zone B hydrocarbon trend
evidence.excerpt: LEL-ZB-04 is rising at 8.4 percent LEL while the normal standalone alarm threshold is 10 percent LEL.

evidence.title: Active Zone B hot-work permit
evidence.excerpt: Permit PTW-HW-204 is active for hot work near pump P-101.

evidence.title: Workers detected near P-101
evidence.excerpt: Structured CCTV event CCTV-ZB-778 reports three workers in the affected work area.

evidence.title: Hot-work atmospheric safety procedure
evidence.excerpt: The retrieved procedure requires supervisory review when ventilation or atmospheric conditions become unsafe during hot work.

similar_incidents.title: Pump seal vapour ignited during nearby hot work
similar_incidents.summary: Hydrocarbon vapour accumulated after ventilation failed and ignited during permitted hot work.
root_causes: The pump seal leak was not isolated before work began
Ventilation failure was not linked to permit suspension
preventive_actions: Correlate gas trends, ventilation state, workers, and active permits

limitations: This prototype has not verified readings against a production refinery instrument system
The retrieved historical incident is synthetic and must not be presented as a real event
